text: Ever wonder why we resist change, even when it’s better? This is status quo bias, a tendency to favor the familiar over the unknown. Studies show it’s not just laziness; it’s wired in our brains. We feel safer sticking with what we know. People often pay more for old habits than exploring new options. We fear loss more than we crave gain. Recognizing this bias empowers us to make better choices and embrace change. Ready to challenge your status quo? Hit like and follow for more insights!
